name: Documentation Pass

tasks.title: Update how-it-works.md for daemon-driven commits
tasks.body:
Update docs/humans/how-it-works.md to reflect that the daemon commits, not the agent. The execution protocol description should describe the daemon's commit-after-every-iteration flow. Remove any references to the agent making commits or running git commands.

Key points to cover:
- The daemon commits after every task iteration (success and failure)
- Commit messages follow the format: 'wolfcastle: <task-id> complete' or 'wolfcastle: <task-id> partial (attempt N)'
- The user's staging area is preserved (daemon uses its own index or stash)
- Commits are configurable via the git config section

tasks.title: Document git config section in configuration.md
tasks.description: Document git config section in configuration.md
tasks.body:
Update docs/humans/configuration.md (and any new config pages created by the config-docs-overhaul project) to document the full git config section:

- git.auto_commit (bool, default: true): master switch for all daemon git operations
- git.commit_on_success (bool, default: true): commit after successful task completion
- git.commit_on_failure (bool, default: true): commit partial work after task failure
- git.commit_state (bool, default: true): include .wolfcastle/ state in commits
- git.skip_hooks (bool, default: false): pass --no-verify to git commit

Explain the hierarchy: auto_commit is the master switch, the others are subordinate. When auto_commit is false, no commits happen regardless of other settings.

Include examples of common configurations:
- Disable all auto-commits: git.auto_commit: false
- Commit on failure only (for manual commit workflows): commit_on_success: false
- Exclude state from commits: commit_state: false

tasks.title: Update collaboration.md and failure-and-recovery.md
tasks.body:
Update docs/humans/collaboration.md:
- Update the git integration section to describe daemon-driven auto-commits
- Explain staging area preservation (users can git add manually without interference)
- Update branch safety / verification content if it references agent commits

Update docs/humans/failure-and-recovery.md:
- Update partial work preservation to describe the new commit-on-failure behavior
- Explain that the daemon auto-commits partial work with a descriptive message
- Describe how auto_commit and commit_on_failure config fields control this behavior

Update README.md:
- Update any references to how commits work
- Keep it concise; point to configuration.md for details

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Documentation Pass is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify no human-facing doc still says the agent commits or runs git commands
